message:
feat(desktop_notify): tick 212 DesktopNotifier::send_with_cx pre-flight cx check (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

DesktopNotifier inherited the NotificationSender trait-default
`send_with_cx` that delegated to `send()` — so cx passed to
`send_with_cx` was silently discarded. The inner `send()` body
uses `std::process::Command::output()` to exec the platform
notification binary (osascript / notify-send / etc.) which is a
SYNCHRONOUS subprocess call that blocks the async executor for
however long the notification backend takes (~100ms typical).

Tick 212 adds a minimal `send_with_cx` override with a pre-flight
`cx.is_cancel_requested()` check. Returns a structured
NotificationDelivery with error = "cancelled before desktop
notification dispatch" when the caller's cx is already cancelled
on entry. Otherwise delegates to `send()`.

The sync Command::output() itself can't observe cx mid-exec; the
pre-flight check is the scope this tick covers. Matches the
pattern used for cass/caut clients' sync-subprocess boundaries.

Without this override, a cancel fired on the notification
pipeline's cx would still run the (blocking) subprocess exec
for every queued desktop alert. After this tick, the exec is
skipped cleanly.

Verification: 104 / 104 desktop_notify:: + notifications:: tests
pass under --features asupersync-runtime via fork bypass
(CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
